channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 6th Sunday after Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 6th Sunday after Pentecost

item.title: 6th Sunday after Pentecost, Healing of the paralytic. , How can a paralytic show faith? , 2004

item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland

item.itunes:summary: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. Healing of the paralytic., The inner life. , 2009-07-19

item.title: 6th Sunday after Pentecost, The Paralytic, A Promise And Rebuke Regarding Faith, 2010-07-04
item.description: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. The healing of the paralytic shows that God hears the prayers of those who have faith. This is both a promise and a rebuke for us. We are responsible before God to have faith so that our prayers for our loved ones are effective. How do we gain more faith?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. The healing of the paralytic shows that God hears the prayers of those who have faith. This is both a promise and a rebuke for us. We are responsible before God to have faith so that our prayers for our loved ones are effective. How do we gain more faith?

item.title: 6th Sunday after Pentecost, Remission Of Sins Of The Paralytic And Woman Who Was A Sinner, 2011-07-24

item.itunes:summary: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. Remission Of Sins Of The Paralytic And Woman Who Was A Sinner, 2011-07-24

item.title: 6th Sunday after Pentecost, Let Love Be Without Dissimulation, A Reason For All The Rules, 2012-07-14
item.description: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. St Paul can be hard to read because his theology is so dense. There is a common pattern to his writings however; he presents his theological arguments, and then proceeds to tell us the "rules". We are in a non-dogmatic age, where there is only one rule - one cannot believe, much less proclaim that there are rules! the Christian life is not this way! Our rules are based on our theology, and are summarized by love. In this selection, St Paul gives a lot of rules, and they are summed up by his admonition: "Let love be without dissimulation." He gives us a reason for following moral rules just preceding this selection: " I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.".
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. St Paul can be hard to read because his theology is so dense. There is a common pattern to his writings however; he presents his theological arguments, and then proceeds to tell us the "rules". We are in a non-dogmatic age, where there is only one rule - one cannot believe, much less proclaim that there are rules! the Christian life is not this way! Our rules are based on our theology, and are summarized by love. In this selection, St Paul gives a lot of rules, and they are summed up by his admonition: "Let love be without dissimulation." He gives us a reason for following moral rules just preceding this selection: " I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.".

item.title: 6th Sunday after Pentecost, They Brought To Him A Man Sick Of The Palsy, Bear Ye One Anothers Burdens And So Fulfil The Law Of Christ, 2013-08-04
item.description: :6th Sunday after Pentecost. They Brought To Him A Man Sick Of The Palsy, Bear Ye One Anothers Burdens And So Fulfil The Law Of Christ, 2013-08-04
